revision.text:
[[de:Fahrt auf dem Land]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|ja|前王朝時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Egypt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Egypt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |NAME = W3-D33:N35-X1:P1-N35:X1-N16
Heb-chenet-net-ta
''Ḥb-ẖnt-nt-t3''
''Journey (as a procession) on land'' |NAME2-ERWEITERT = '''Festival date''' |NAME2 = N12:Z1*Z1*Z1*Z1:Z1*Z1*Z1*Z1:N5-N11:Z1*Z1*Z1 O1-D21:X1 |NAME2-TRANSKRIPTION =''3ḫt'' |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = ''Eighth day of the moon in the third month of the period {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'' |BILD1 = Maquette-barque-khéops.jpg |BILD1-BREITE = 300px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = {{ill|Khufu|en|Khufu|qid=Q161904|lt=Khufu|1=Khufu}} {{ill|Watercraft|en|Watercraft|qid=Q1229765|lt=barque|1=Watercraft}} as falcon barque }} The {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|qid=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt|1=Ancient Egypt}} festival '''Journey on Land''' ('''Chenet-net-ta''') is attested since the {{ill|Predynastic Egypt|ja|前王朝時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Egypt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Egypt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Predynastic Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in connection with the {{ill|Horus Procession|ja|ホルスの随行|de|Horusgeleit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus Procession|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus Procession|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus Procession|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and thus counts among the oldest festivals in the ancient Egyptian festival calendar. == Background == === The festival "Journey on Land" === Originally, the king traveled through the land every two years to administer justice and distribute gifts for outstanding achievements of the population. These activities were accompanied by the festival "Journey on Land." In early times, village leaders and minor princes were responsible for supplying the king. Standards were carried along with military divisions accompanying the king throughout the land. In this context, the military force was also considered the escort of Horus. The associated royal motif of "Punishment of the wicked and reward of the loyal" persisted in various forms until the {{ill|Late Period of ancient Egypt|ja|古代エジプト第26王朝以降|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period of ancient Egypt|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In tomb paintings associated with the ceremonies, a ship was consistently depicted. This form of representation is found in {{ill|Archaeology|en|Archaeology|qid=Q23498|lt=archaeology|1=Archaeology}} findings during the {{ill|Naqada II period|ja|ナカダII期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naqada II period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naqada II period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naqada II period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} throughout Egypt. Initially, the ship served primarily as a “container of gifts and offerings.” For this purpose, the royal ship represented "Horus, the royal falcon" as a divine symbol of the king himself. The gift vessels probably contained ointments and {{ill|sacred oils|ja|聖油|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred oils|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred oils|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred oils|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which the loyal people of the land received as divine gifts. Even in later times, ointments and oils continued to have great importance as sacred offerings. Offerings from the population could, in return, be stored in the now-empty ship. The king visited several stops along the {{ill|Nile|en|Nile|qid=Q3392|lt=Nile|1=Nile}} with the ship on which the Horus Procession stood in the form of divine statues in small {{ill|Hokora|en|Hokora|qid=Q1442984|lt=shrines|1=Hokora}}.{{ill|Björn Landström|ja|ビョルン・ランドストローム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Björn Landström|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Björn Landström|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Björn Landström|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Ships of the Pharaohs – 4000 years of Egyptian shipbuilding.'' Allen & Unwin, London 1970, p. 120. While the people celebrated on the banks of the Nile, the king acted in his role as divine judge. As a symbol of jurisdiction, a judgment pole with the execution device "Shemsi" mounted on it was affixed at the front of the ship, which the king later replaced with the “Pole of the World Ruler Re” after the introduction of the Re cult. For example, the forty-meter-long royal ship of {{ill|Khufu|en|Khufu|qid=Q161904|lt=Khufu|1=Khufu}} was covered in {{ill|gold|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|lt=Metal (金)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|1=Metal (wuxing)|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|lt=Metal (金)|lt=Metal (金)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gold|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gold|13=User:Immanuelle/Metal (wuxing)|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|lt=Metal (金)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gold|13=User:Immanuelle/Metal (wuxing)|ja|Metal (金)|1=Metal (wuxing)|lt=Metal (金)|2=es|3=Metal (Wu Xing)|4=it|5=Metallo (elemento)|6=vi|7=Kim (Ngũ hành)|8=tr|9=Metal (klasik element)|10=uk|11=Метал (У-сін)|lt=Metal (金)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and served as the "{{ill|great house of judgment|ja|裁きの大殿|de|Rechtsprechung|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/great house of judgment|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/great house of judgment|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/great house of judgment|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." === Al-Lahun Papyri === As the festival is listed on different days in the {{ill|Egyptian calendar|ja|エジプト暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian calendar|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦法|1=rekihou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11514955}} of the month {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja|ペレト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} III, the festival dates are based on the {{ill|lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|ja|古代エジプトの太陰暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the {{ill|Papyrus Berlin 10003B VS|ja|パピルス・ベルリン10003B VS|de|Papyrus Berlin 10003B VS|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Berlin 10003B VS|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Berlin 10003B VS|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Berlin 10003B VS|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 17th Peret III is mentioned as the festival day for the ninth regnal year of {{ill|Senusret III|ja|センウセルト3世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret III|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to センウセレト3世}}. The reading and identification are problematic. Rolf Krauss considers it possible that this could be the festival day of the “Journey through the land,” which falls on the eighth day of the lunar calendar. == Literature == * {{ill|Rainer Hannig|ja|ライナー・ハンニッヒ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rainer Hannig|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rainer Hannig|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rainer Hannig|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Großes Handwörterbuch Ägyptisch-Deutsch. (2800–950 v. Chr.)''. von Zabern, Mainz 2006, ISBN 3-8053-1771-9. p. 686. * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens.'' Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリート・ショット|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491464|1=Siegfried Schott}}: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz / Wiesbaden 1950. == References == {{translated page|de|Fahrt auf dem Land|version=246265801|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Fahrt auf dem Land]] [[ja:陸上の旅]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{wikidata link|Q1392689}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:Ancient_Egyptian_festivals]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
